description_long: This is a classic biplane with two parallel wings stacked one above the other, connected by struts. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers towards the tail, which features a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer. The cockpit is positioned near the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed with a clear canopy. The airplane is equipped with two visible propeller engines mounted on the lower wing, one on each side of the fuselage. The landing gear is not clearly visible, but the overall design suggests a vintage or early 20th-century style. The exterior is finished in a combination of dark and metallic colors, giving it a sleek, polished appearance.
description: A vintage-style biplane with dual stacked wings, a streamlined fuselage, and two propeller engines mounted on the lower wing. The cockpit is enclosed and positioned at the front, with a polished dark and metallic finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual propellers, stacked wings, and the enclosed cockpit at the nose.
description_view_1: Angled top-side view highlighting the upper and lower wings, engine placement, and the streamlined fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with a single vertical stabilizer and the full span of the wings.
description_view_3: Angled top-side view from the opposite direction, showing the cockpit, wing structure, and tail configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the lower wing, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not clearly visible; configuration indeterminate from images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely single-seat.
